name: Southern Maryland Stuffed Ham
recipeInstructions:
1. Choose a plump, thick ham with a thin layer of fat.
2. Parboil the ham for 20 minutes.
3. Steam and remove all skin from the ham. Cut greens into small pieces (about 2 inches).
4. Chop celery and hot red pepper finely.
5. Blanch greens, celery, and hot red pepper in boiling water until limp; drain well.
6. Add seasonings and mix thoroughly.
7. Cool the ham until it can be handled comfortably.
8. Starting at the butt end of the ham, cut three lengthwise slits, each about 2 inches long, all the way through.
- Second row: two slits
- Third row: three slits (so that one slit for stuffing does not split into another)
9. Use your fingers to push the seasoned greens into the slits first from the top, then from underneath, until all spaces are filled.
10. Bank any remaining greens over the top of the ham.
11. Place a large square of clean cloth in the ham boiler.
12. Lay the ham, skin side up, on the cloth and fold the ends tightly, pinning securely.
13. Return to the ham boiler and simmer until tender (about 15 minutes per pound).
14. Let cool for two hours in the pot liquor before refrigerating overnight, wrapped in the cloth.
